bug_data.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
bug_data.description:
We haven't used this in a while. (I'm guessing since e10s shipped, because EGL images cannot be sent cross-process.) But since accelerated canvas and webgl live in the GPU process nowadays, we can in theory use it again. It avoids the glitches in bug 1892716, for example.

It has bitrotted somewhat, so some work needs to be done to SharedSurface_EGLImage and (Render)EGLImageTextureHost to get it to work.

comments.text:
Created attachment 9403359
Bug 1898238 - Don't call TexTypeForWebgl when initializing offscreen canvas. r?aosmond,jgilbert


This is only used if the OffscreenCanvas has a webgl context, in which
case it forwards the tex type to WebGLContext::PresentFrontBuffer(). A
later patch in this series will make TexTypeForWebgl() return a
different value depending on whether the webgl context is in-process
or remote, which is something that cannot be known when the
OffscreenCanvas is initialized. This patch therefore removes the field
from OffscreenCanvas and makes WebGLContext::PresentFrontBuffer() call
TexTypeForWebgl() itself.

comments.raw_text:
HandleExternalImage() has two nearly identical code paths for
rendering either RenderAndroidSurfaceTextureHost or
RenderAndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost using CompositorOGL. The next
patch in this series will additionally add support for
RenderEGLImageTextureHost. Rather than duplicate the code yet again,
we abstract the differences in to a new virtual function
RenderTextureHost::CreateTextureSource() and use a single common code
path in HandleExternalImage().
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.text:
Created attachment 9403360
Bug 1898238 - Tidy up RenderCompositorOGLSWGL::HandleExternalImage. r?sotaro


HandleExternalImage() has two nearly identical code paths for
rendering either RenderAndroidSurfaceTextureHost or
RenderAndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost using CompositorOGL. The next
patch in this series will additionally add support for
RenderEGLImageTextureHost. Rather than duplicate the code yet again,
we abstract the differences in to a new virtual function
RenderTextureHost::CreateTextureSource() and use a single common code
path in HandleExternalImage().

Created attachment 9403361
Bug 1898238 - Make SharedSurface_EGLImage and (Render)EGLImageTextureHost work. r?sotaro


SharedSurface_EGLImage has mostly avoided bitrotting. However, it now
sometimes gets destructed after the GLContext has been lost, resulting
in a crash when attempting to clean up its resources. To avoid this,
we hold a weak reference to the EglDisplay, and use that to clean up
rather than the GLContext.

To support rendering with SWGL, EGLImageTextureHost ensures the
correct preferCompositorSurface, supportsExternalCompositing, and
imageType values are sent to webrender. RenderEGLImageTextureHost now
implements RenderTextureHostSWGL, as well as the new
CreateTextureSource() function, and EGLImageTextureSource is
initialized with a GL context reference so that it may actually work.

Additionally we use eglWaitSync() rather than eglClientWaitSync()
where available for improved performance. And we ensure that we bind
the EGL images' textures to GL_TEXTURE_2D rather than
GL_TEXTURE_EXTERNAL when running on the Android emulator to work
around the buggy implementation in some emulator versions.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com

comments.text:
Created attachment 9403362
Bug 1898238 - Keep SharedSurface_EGLImages alive but do not recycle them. r?sotaro


We must ensure that a SharedSurface_EGLImage outlives its
corresponding RenderEGLImageTextureHost, so that
RenderEGLImageTextureHost does not attempt to wait on an EGLSync that
has been destroyed. This can be achieved by passing the surface to
RemoteTextureOwnerClient::PushTexture(), ensuring the RemoteTextureMap
keeps it alive.

However, we must be careful not to recycle the SharedSurface_EGLImage,
as that is currently not supported. To achieve this, we make
RemoteTextureMap::RecycleTexture() only recycle SharedSurfaces whose
mDesc.canRecycle value is true.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com

However, we must be careful not to recycle the SharedSurface_EGLImage,
as that is currently not supported. To achieve this, we make

mDesc.canRecycle value is true.

Adds a pref wegb.enable-egl-image which is true by default. When
enabled, we use EGL images as the preferred texture type for
out-of-process webgl and accelerated canvas on Android. In-process
webgl will never attempt to use EGL images, regardless of the value of
the pref, as we are unable to send EGL images cross-process.

comments.text:
We haven't used this in a while. (I'm guessing since e10s shipped, because EGL images cannot be sent cross-process.) But since accelerated canvas and webgl live in the GPU process nowadays, we can in theory use it again. It avoids the glitches in bug 1892716, for example.

It has bitrotted somewhat, so some work needs to be done to SharedSurface_EGLImage and (Render)EGLImageTextureHost to get it to work.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
We haven't used this in a while. (I'm guessing since e10s shipped, because EGL images cannot be sent cross-process.) But since accelerated canvas and webgl live in the GPU process nowadays, we can in theory use it again. It avoids the glitches in bug 1892716, for example.

It has bitrotted somewhat, so some work needs to be done to SharedSurface_EGLImage and (Render)EGLImageTextureHost to get it to work.

This is only used if the OffscreenCanvas has a webgl context, in which
case it forwards the tex type to WebGLContext::PresentFrontBuffer(). A
later patch in this series will make TexTypeForWebgl() return a
different value depending on whether the webgl context is in-process
or remote, which is something that cannot be known when the
OffscreenCanvas is initialized. This patch therefore removes the field
from OffscreenCanvas and makes WebGLContext::PresentFrontBuffer() call
TexTypeForWebgl() itself.

Created attachment 9403360
Bug 1898238 - Tidy up RenderCompositorOGLSWGL::HandleExternalImage. r?sotaro


HandleExternalImage() has two nearly identical code paths for
rendering either RenderAndroidSurfaceTextureHost or
RenderAndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost using CompositorOGL. The next
patch in this series will additionally add support for
RenderEGLImageTextureHost. Rather than duplicate the code yet again,
we abstract the differences in to a new virtual function
RenderTextureHost::CreateTextureSource() and use a single common code
path in HandleExternalImage().

comments.text:
Created attachment 9403361
Bug 1898238 - Make SharedSurface_EGLImage and (Render)EGLImageTextureHost work. r?sotaro


SharedSurface_EGLImage has mostly avoided bitrotting. However, it now
sometimes gets destructed after the GLContext has been lost, resulting
in a crash when attempting to clean up its resources. To avoid this,
we hold a weak reference to the EglDisplay, and use that to clean up
rather than the GLContext.

To support rendering with SWGL, EGLImageTextureHost ensures the
correct preferCompositorSurface, supportsExternalCompositing, and
imageType values are sent to webrender. RenderEGLImageTextureHost now
implements RenderTextureHostSWGL, as well as the new
CreateTextureSource() function, and EGLImageTextureSource is
initialized with a GL context reference so that it may actually work.

Additionally we use eglWaitSync() rather than eglClientWaitSync()
where available for improved performance. And we ensure that we bind
the EGL images' textures to GL_TEXTURE_2D rather than
GL_TEXTURE_EXTERNAL when running on the Android emulator to work
around the buggy implementation in some emulator versions.

sometimes gets destructed after the GLContext has been lost, resulting
in a crash when attempting to clean up its resources. To avoid this,
we hold a weak reference to the EglDisplay, and use that to clean up
rather than the GLContext.

To support rendering with SWGL, EGLImageTextureHost ensures the
correct preferCompositorSurface, supportsExternalCompositing, and
imageType values are sent to webrender. RenderEGLImageTextureHost now
implements RenderTextureHostSWGL, as well as the new
CreateTextureSource() function, and EGLImageTextureSource is
initialized with a GL context reference so that it may actually work.

Additionally we use eglWaitSync() rather than eglClientWaitSync()

around the buggy implementation in some emulator versions.

comments.raw_text:
We must ensure that a SharedSurface_EGLImage outlives its
corresponding RenderEGLImageTextureHost, so that
RenderEGLImageTextureHost does not attempt to wait on an EGLSync that
has been destroyed. This can be achieved by passing the surface to
RemoteTextureOwnerClient::PushTexture(), ensuring the RemoteTextureMap
keeps it alive.

However, we must be careful not to recycle the SharedSurface_EGLImage,
as that is currently not supported. To achieve this, we make
RemoteTextureMap::RecycleTexture() only recycle SharedSurfaces whose
mDesc.canRecycle value is true.
comments.text:
Created attachment 9403362
Bug 1898238 - Keep SharedSurface_EGLImages alive but do not recycle them. r?sotaro


We must ensure that a SharedSurface_EGLImage outlives its
corresponding RenderEGLImageTextureHost, so that
RenderEGLImageTextureHost does not attempt to wait on an EGLSync that
has been destroyed. This can be achieved by passing the surface to
RemoteTextureOwnerClient::PushTexture(), ensuring the RemoteTextureMap
keeps it alive.

However, we must be careful not to recycle the SharedSurface_EGLImage,
as that is currently not supported. To achieve this, we make
RemoteTextureMap::RecycleTexture() only recycle SharedSurfaces whose
mDesc.canRecycle value is true.

comments.text:
Created attachment 9403363
Bug 1898238 - Use SharedSurface_EGLImage for out-of-process webgl and accelerated canvas on Android. r?sotaro,jgilbert


Adds a pref wegb.enable-egl-image which is true by default. When
enabled, we use EGL images as the preferred texture type for
out-of-process webgl and accelerated canvas on Android. In-process
webgl will never attempt to use EGL images, regardless of the value of
the pref, as we are unable to send EGL images cross-process.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com

comments.author: nfay@mozilla.com

bug_analysis.root_cause: EGL images could not be used cross-process, blocking usage with out-of-process webgl and accelerated canvas on Android. Since these now run in the GPU process, it became possible to re-enable SharedSurface_EGLImage usage after addressing bitrot and cleanup issues.
symptoms: Glitches in accelerated canvas and webgl rendering on Android
Crashes due to SharedSurface_EGLImage destruction after GLContext loss
reproduction_steps: Use out-of-process WebGL or accelerated canvas on Android
Observe rendering glitches and/or crashes related to EGL image handling
bug_analysis.technical_details: The patch series updates SharedSurface_EGLImage and RenderEGLImageTextureHost to work correctly with the GPU process on Android by holding weak references to EglDisplay instead of GLContext, preventing crashes. It abstracts texture host creation to handle EGLImage textures, and adds a pref (webgl.enable-egl-image) to enable EGL images for out-of-process WebGL and accelerated canvas. In-process WebGL does not use EGL images due to cross-process limitations.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Improved rendering stability and visual correctness for accelerated canvas and WebGL content on Android devices, reducing glitches and crashes.

refined_analysis.root_cause: EGLImages cannot be shared across processes on Android, causing rendering glitches and crashes due to stale GLContext references in out-of-process WebGL and accelerated canvas.
symptoms: Rendering glitches in accelerated canvas and WebGL on Android
Crashes during SharedSurface_EGLImage destruction after GLContext loss
reproduction_steps: Run out-of-process WebGL or accelerated canvas on Android

refined_analysis.technical_details: Refactor SharedSurface_EGLImage and RenderEGLImageTextureHost classes to replace direct GLContext references with weak EglDisplay references to prevent crashes after GLContext loss. Update texture host creation to support EGLImage textures. Introduce a 'webgl.enable-egl-image' pref to toggle EGLImage support for out-of-process WebGL and accelerated canvas on Android. In-process WebGL remains unchanged due to cross-process EGLImage limitations.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Improves stability and rendering correctness of accelerated canvas and WebGL on Android by eliminating EGLImage-related crashes and rendering glitches.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: 1) Refactor SharedSurface_EGLImage (gfx/gl/SharedSurfaceEGL.cpp/h) and RenderEGLImageTextureHost (gfx/layers/RenderEGLImageTextureHost.cpp/h) to hold weak references to EglDisplay instead of GLContext. 2) Modify texture host creation to support EGLImage textures. 3) Add 'webgl.enable-egl-image' pref to enable EGLImage support for out-of-process WebGL and accelerated canvas on Android.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: On Android, out-of-process WebGL and accelerated canvas crash or glitch due to cross-process EGLImage limitations and stale GLContext references in gfx/gl/SharedSurfaceEGL.cpp/h and gfx/layers/RenderEGLImageTextureHost.cpp/h. Refactor these to use weak EglDisplay references and add a 'webgl.enable-egl-image' pref to enable EGLImage support, fixing rendering issues and crashes.
